text.text: A material that provides some EXP for leveling up Lv.1-60 operators.
A storage device with combat video recordings. New operators use this to acquire combat knowledge.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A material that provides decent EXP for leveling up Lv.1-60 operators.

A storage device with combat video recordings. New operators can use this to acquire combat knowledge. The end of each recording also features interviews conducted with operators who excelled in various training exercises.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash
A material that provides a lot of EXP for leveling up Lv.1-60 operators

A storage device with combat video recordings. New operators can use this to acquire combat knowledge. This is also furnished with simulations of historical battles so that viewers can better appreciate the brutal nature of actual wars fought upon Talos-II.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Gives it to operators whose hobbies are [Sports] and/or [Nature] to build trust with them.
Builds even more trust when given to operators who like [Wuling Runners' Choice].
Civilian variant of Yinglung training dumbbells. Furnished with an on-board weight manipulator. Automatically adjusts the weight to fine tune your resistance training.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Gives it to operators whose hobbies are [Lifestyle] and/or [Culture] to build trust with them.
Builds even more trust when given to operators who like [Wuling Runners' Choice].
Special grade tea leaves offered by Eureka Teashop. The emerging brand is really proud of this product. Comes with a designer package that can be assembled into a mini-tea table.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Gives it to operators whose hobbies are [Technology] and/or [Charity] to build trust with them.
Builds even more trust when given to operators who like [Valley Protocol's Choice].
A popular science periodical in the Civilization Band. Recent issues of the magazine are focusing on the pioneering technologies of Endfield Industries.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Gives it to operators whose hobbies are [Fine Arts] and/or [Lifestyle] to build trust with them.
Builds even more trust when given to operators who like [Valley Industry's Choice].
A shawl woven from soft, fine fibers. The craftmakers of Simonch treat these shawls as a symbol of friendship. They would make wonderful gifts for your friends.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Gives it to operators whose hobbies are [Nature] and/or [Culture] to build trust with them.
Builds even more trust when given to operators who like [Valley Protocol's Choice].

A beautiful crystalline stone found during humanity's earliest attempts to explore the seas of Talos-II. Some of them have entered the market and processed into various collector's items. The fact that such beautiful items exist in the depths of Talosian seas is extremely exciting.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Gives it to operators whose hobbies are [Creative] and/or [Fashion] to build trust with them.
Builds even more trust when given to operators who like [Valley Protocol's Choice].
Intricately packaged perfume. The primary ingredients are extracts of plants growing on the snowcapped peaks of Kjersch. The fragrance is a reminder of refreshing mountain breeze.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Gives it to operators whose hobbies are [Sports] and/or [Fine Arts] to build trust with them.
Builds even more trust when given to operators who like [Valley Protocol's Choice].
An entry ticket to the La Fantoma Surfing Festival. Do not forget to bring your own surfing board when you head over to the competition.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Gives it to operators whose hobbies are [Educational] and/or [Culture] to build trust with them.
Builds even more trust when given to operators who like [Valley Industry's Choice].

A toy designed according to key components of the Cosmic Gate. These puzzles come in all sorts of assembling difficulties. Legends say those who complete the most challenging puzzles may have a chance of repairing the Cosmic Gate itself.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Gives it to operators whose hobbies are [Technology] and/or [Nature] to build trust with them.
Builds even more trust when given to operators who like [Valley Industry's Choice].
"The Blight may melt the world, but your individuality remains as keen as before. Choose Nikolas 006C. Connect our vision to the future."
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Use this at the Control Nexus aboard
Dijiang
to unlock the next expansion phase of the entire vessel.
This data chip should contain plans for expanding
Dijiang
but its memory array only contains vast amounts of incredibly encrypted code... Someone might know how to decipher this, but who might that be?
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Unlocks the next phase of the Wuling AIC Plan
A special device containing environmental data of the Wuling region. Connect this to the Automation-Core to expand its data processing capabilities.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Unlocks the next phase of the Basic AIC Plan
A special device containing environmental data of Valley IV. Connect this to the Automation-Core to expand its data processing capabilities.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A currency issued in Wuling and only usable in the said region. It has all sorts of uses.

Pursuant to relevant provisions of the Band Accord, all Accordatories shall, while ensuring the unimpeded circulation of Talosian Credit Notes in the civilian economy, maintain commodity stockpiles to cover the issuance of such stock bills, thereby facilitating orderly and effective joint stockpile redistribution under exceptional circumstances.
A currency issued in Valley IV and only usable in the said region. It has all sorts of uses

Pursuant to relevant provisions of the Band Accord, all signatories shall, in addition to simultaneously guaranteeing that Talosian Credit Notes provide a currency circulation sufficient for the civil economy, guarantee a commodity stockpile sufficient for the agreed-upon supply of such types of allocation orders so as to achieve an orderly and effective joint allocation during extraordinary circumstances.
These index points are required to research various AIC technologies in the Wuling AIC Plan.
A small electrical device that records AIC Factory performance in the Wuling region. Connect it to the Automation-Core to adjust Techno-Protocol deployment balance.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash
These index points are required to research various AIC technologies in the Basic AIC Plan.
A small electrical device that records AIC Factory performance in Valley IV. Connect it to the PAC to adjust Techno-Protocol deployment balance.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
An advanced industrial construction material. Can be used to upgrade
Dijiang
If this material exhibits any sign of warping or discoloration, IMMEDIATELY CONTACT the Endministrator or Supervisor Perlica.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A basic construction material. Can be used to upgrade
Dijiang
Only use these materials within the effective range of Protocol facilities.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant DEF
. Duration:
s.
A mysterious Ursine chef came up with this recipe as he traveled across the lands. It became famous throughout the frontier for its delicious blend of sweetness and heat.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant ATK
and Critical Rate
. Duration
s.

A herbal meal prepared by stewing redjade ginseng roots, meat fillet, and other ingredients. A common winter dish of the Wuling region. When the snow starts falling, a bowl of this hearty stew would offer the warmth you need.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to
dispel
Arts Inflictions
and
Arts Reactions
, then instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.

Cubes of meat stir-fried over hot fire. A hungry diner can easily go through three bowls of rice with a serving of meat stir-fry. There's a good reason why this is the *most* popular dish in the frontier.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.
A meat pie. Everyone in Valley IV grew up eating the famous Valley pie. Steaming pies, the stove, and a loving family always bring people hope.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant Critical Rate
. Duration:
s.

Meatballs dyed in a way to make them resemble Xiranite. Although the resemblance is close enough to make diners hesitate, the tenderness, fragrance, and flavors are guaranteed to conquer every foodie.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant DEF
and DMG Reduction
against all forms of DMG. Duration:
s.
A generous and colorful dish. No one cares how the ingredient is cut or stir-fried, or how strong the fire is. Every Wuling household has their own Flame-Boyance approach.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant DMG reduction
against all forms of DMG. Duration:
s.

A dish that poses a serious challenge to the chef. Quillbeast livers can quickly overcook and assume a texture similar to rubbery leather. A skilled cook must first come up with a way to give it the same tender crisp of young bamboo sprouts.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
Max HP per second plus
HP. Duration
s.

These jellied greens are bouncy and jiggly to the touch. Special molds were used by the manufacturers to create jellied greens shaped like Chubby Lung. It is the only option since everyone likes Chubby Lungs so much. Kyaaa! Chubby Lung! They're so cute!
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to
dispel
Arts Inflictions
and
Arts Reactions
, then restore
HP per second for
s.
Slug meat that has been completely dehydrated. The pulled jerky is incredibly chewy. This is one of the best pet foods in Valley IV.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this for the entire team to restore
Max HP per second plus
HP. Duration:
s.

Fruit preserves with a sour and spicy flavor. Unripened citromes can be extremely sour, but cut them to pieces (along with the rind) and marinate them in a blend of tartpeppers and aggagrit condiments. The result is a refreshing riot of sour and spicy.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this for the entire team to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.

An extremely hard and salty tack. After the decision to construct the Hub Base was made, the first Endfield operators arriving at Valley IV considered the possibility of supply shortage. Hence, they specifically added these emergency rations known for their extremely long shelf lives to the supply list.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.
Fried rice made with less oil and salt to bring out the wholesome flavors of garden greens and vegetables.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant Critical Rate
and All Damage
. Duration
s.

A herbal infusion prepared by decocting redjade ginseng and jincao herbs. Typically consumed to revitalize the body. Many grievously wounded warriors used this decoction to treat various chronic conditions.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.
A uniquely flavored meat stew made using Anthony's family recipe. A wildland wanderer like Anthony often carried this chili along with him. It reminds him of home and gives him the calories he needs.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant DMG Reduction
against all forms of DMG. Duration
s.
A pain relief salve prepared using Karja's secret formula. The salve is very popular amongst those engaged with heavy manual labor.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to
dispel
Arts Inflictions
and
Arts Reactions
, then restore
Max HP plus
HP.
A stimulating tisane prepared using Karja's secret formula. Don't drink it in daytime, though.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant DEF
. Duration:
s.
A comfort food with comforting heat. It is an ancient legacy passed from one planet to another. It tastes of home.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant ATK
and Critical Rate
. Duration
s.
An extremely simple snack. For expatriates who cannot return home ... a quick fix of nostalgia can be acquired in the most simple of sweets.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant DEF
, DMG Reduction
against all forms of DMG, and Treatment Bonus
. Duration
s.
Pop it open and start chugging. Feel the fizz dance on your tongue, then give a satisfied burp. It's that simple.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
Max HP per second plus
HP. Duration
s.
A jelly that people can easily prepare, even in the wildlands. Citromes can be dissolved in a mixture of two different types of aggagrits to create a strange jelly-like material.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant Physical DMG Dealt
. Duration
s.

Getting an authentic Mama John's burger is not easy for explorations operating in the field. But if you try hard enough, you can make something that is at least eighty-percent similar to the Mama's famous sandwiches.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant DMG Reduction
against all forms of DMG. Duration
s.

Despite looking edible, we do not recommend eating this. This is a smoked "plant" prepared by using hazefyre-tainted jerky in place of sawdust and special aggagrit to replace sugar. Only the "bravest of the brave" would dare try it.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant ATK
, Critical Rate
, and All Damage
. Duration
s.
A saucy condiment with plenty of aggagrit powder in it. Another ingredient in its preparation would be the chromatic lipids used by Bonekrusher Arsonists as fuel for their flamethrowers.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this for the entire team to restore
Max HP per second plus
HP. Duration
s.

Savory cookies with bits of cartilaginous material inside it. Look cute but have an extremely dry and gagging taste. This recipe was rumored to be a Landbreaker creation. It would seem that these raiders are actually capable of focusing on the details. A rare occasion indeed.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant ATK
. Duration
s.

Bread with a hardness akin to D64 Steel. The origins can be traced back to the time before the Landbreakers splintered into separate clans. According to the Landbreakers, the legendary warlord Jakub always had this on his dining table.
This is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant ATK
. Duration:
s.

A meat soup with a fruity flavor. What did people eat on the first day of constructing the Hub Base? There are plenty of stories around, but many veterans claimed they had a fruity meat stew when they turned the sod.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this for the entire team to instantly gain
Ultimate Energy.

Citrome-flavored pudding with balanced sour and sweet flavors. Bits of aggagrit were added during boiling. Originium Slug extracts were then used to solidify the entire thing. This cloyingly sweet aroma is simply ... magnificent.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly gain
Ultimate Energy.

A basin full of "criminally generous riches". It is obvious that this is prepared with more than one type of meat. Help yourself to a bowl or two. Such normal-looking food is a rarity, considering it was made in a hostile environment.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this for the entire team to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.

A soup mix of aggagrit seasoning and special bone meal. Just add hot water. Despite its savory and wholesome flavors, tasters can't help but weirdly fantasize if the soup mix is just as good "without the bone meal".
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant All Attributes
. Duration:
s.
A wholly black fillet with bits of bone inside it. Seš'qamam cuisine? Probably not. This feels more like a Landbreakers' fantasy about Seš'qamam food.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant DEF
. Duration:
s.
A candy with a sad, greenish color. Its preparation might be based on Landbreaker recipes. It tastes like licorice, but with an added dash of tartness.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.
Despite the absolute lack of meat, this dish has a rich, hearty taste. Aggagrit seasoning may indeed revolutionize certain approaches to the culinary arts.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant DMG Dealt
for all forms of DMG. Duration:
s.

A booster packaged in a metal canister imbued with special Originium Arts. It contains sharp micro-clusters that can activate the user's vitals, excite the nervous system, and greatly accelerate adrenaline secretion.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly revive an unconscious team member to
Max HP.
A booster packaged in a metallic tube imbued with Originium Arts. The contents activate the user's vitals, heighten the nervous system, and give a huge adrenaline boost.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant Ultimate Gain Efficiency
. Duration:
s.
A medicine prepared by Linnaeus. It is infused with his personal emotions and has a perplexing yet rich blend of flavors.
This is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to grant DMG Dealt
for all forms of DMG. Duration:
s.
A booster packaged in a vial imbued with special Originium Arts. It contains sharp micro-clusters that can activate the user's vitals, excite the nervous system, and accelerate adrenaline secretion.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly revive an unconscious team member to
Max HP.
A booster packaged in a vial imbued with Originium Arts. The contents activate the user's vitals, heighten the nervous system, and give an adrenaline boost.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
Max HP per second plus
HP. Duration:
s.
A simple spray made with a filling unit. Administer orally if necessary.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.
An herbal infusion made with simple brewing. Anyone who could chug down a bottle in one go can be regarded as a true "hero".
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
Max HP per second plus
HP. Duration:
s.
A blended mix made with simple filling. This one offers larger dosage and better ingredients compared to its basic counterpart.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.
A pharmaceutical preparation made with simple processing. This one offers a larger dosage and better ingredients compared to its basic counterpart.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
Max HP per second plus
HP. Duration:
s.
A spray that can be made with crafting. Yazhen extracts make excellent insect repellents.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.
A traditional tisane made by brewing a special blend of secret Wuling herbs. The bitter taste will help you recall the saddest and most desperate times of your life.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
Max HP per second plus
HP. Duration:
s.
A blended mix made with simple filling. This one offers a larger dosage compared to its basic counterpart.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
Max HP per second plus
HP. Duration:
s.
A blended mix made with simple filling. It has a very invigorating aroma of citromes.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.
A bottle of medicinal pills made with crafting. This one offers a larger dosage compared to its basic counterpart.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.
A bottle of ingestible medicinal pills made with crafting.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
HP per second for
s.

A can of citromes made with fruits grown in natural and wholesome orchards. Fortified with small amounts of the essential vitamin suite. However, suggested retail price seems to be higher than its worth.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
HP.
A capsule prepared using optimized packaging techniques. This Endfield product was designed for ease-of-use in combat scenarios.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
HP per second for
s.
Can only be administered via subcutaneous injection.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
HP.
A carbonated beverage with a dash of jincao herbal extract. The refreshing aroma made this a popular drink.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
HP per second for
s.
Citrome now comes with revolutionary packaging. However, this was poorly received in the Civilization Band due to its excessive sugar content.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
HP per second for
s.
A cheap can of citromes. This serves as an accessible source of vitamins for settlers in the wildlands.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
HP.

A capsule prepared using the processes of standard pharmaceutical packaging. Endfield Industries once tried to market this but the amount sold barely came to a fraction of the competing Rhodes Island product.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
HP.
A simple capsule made with crafting. It is quite leaky, and shaking it around might reduce its potency.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
Max HP per second plus
HP. Duration:
s.
Yazhen plant can be shredded to a powder form. This must then be dissolved and packaged in a container for easier use.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.
Jincao plant can be shredded to a powder form. This must then be dissolved and packaged in a container for easier use.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
Max HP per second plus
HP. Duration:
s.
A simple blended preparation that can be consumed by mixing with some water. If you're unlucky, you might get some bitter bits of rind in your pack.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
Max HP plus
HP.
A simple powdered preparation of Firebuckle. You can use it to treat wounds, but its pharmaceutical potential goes way beyond that.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
HP per second for
s.
Yazhen plant can be shredded to a powder form. This must then be dissolved and packaged in a container for easier use.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
HP.
Jincao plant can be shredded to a powder form. This must then be dissolved and packaged in a container for easier use.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to restore
HP per second for
s.
Shredding would greatly improve the flavors of Citrome.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this to instantly restore
HP.
Shredding would greatly improve the pharmaceutical effectiveness of Buckflower.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A packaged explosive.
Can be used to directly attack targets. The resulting explosion deals DMG to all nearby enemies.
This is a dangerous explosive. Carry and use with care.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A battery packaged by AIC Factory facilities. It can be used to supply power, charge up facilities, or traded at various outposts.
Xiranite Power is a key project jointly pursued by Wuling and Endfield Industries.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A battery packaged by AIC Factory facilities. It can be used to supply power, charge up facilities, or traded at various outposts.
Many wildlands settlers make a living by stripping waste batteries of recyclable materials.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A battery packaged by AIC Factory facilities. It can be used to supply power, charge up facilities, or traded at various outposts.
A skilled artisan can easily convert a standard capacity (SC) battery into an improvised explosive weapon.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A battery packaged by AIC Factory facilities. It can be used to supply power, charge up facilities, or traded at various outposts.
Use and handle with care. Contents are hazardous to health. Compromising casing integrity may cause the said contents to leak.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A device part made by processing Steel. Can be used to produce other items or construct other facilities.
A Steel Part with a wide range of uses.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A device part made by processing Cryston Fibers. Can be used to produce other items or construct other facilities.
A Cryston Part with a wide range of uses.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A device part made by processing Ferrium. Can be used to produce other items or construct other facilities.
A Ferrium Part with a wide range of uses.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A device part made by processing Amethyst Fibers. Can be used to produce other items or construct other facilities.
An Amethyst part with a wide range of uses.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A metallic bottle made by moulding Steel. Can be used to produce other items.
A bottle made of steel, the most common of its kind in the market.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A metallic bottle made by processing Cryston Fibers. Can be used to produce other items.

Special chemical reagents can only be properly stored with Cryston Bottles. An example would be the notorious aqua mordax, otherwise known as the "corroding water" or localized ferric chloride solvent.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A metallic bottle made by processing Ferrium. Can be used to produce other items.
A metallic bottle rarely seen in the market. Although ferrium metal offers better resistance against oxidation, people still prefer the steel variant.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A bottle made by processing Amethyst Fibers. Can be used to produce other materials.
Amethyst bottles are being phased out from markets in the Civilization Band due to its fragility, weight, and Blight resistance.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Acquired by grinding Ferrium Powder. Can be used to produce other items.
Techno-Protocol grinding is used to make this preparation of dense Ferrium powder. It is extremely popular amongst traditional artists.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Acquired by grinding Amethyst Powder. Can be used to produce other materials.
Techno-Protocol grinding is used to make this Cryston preparation. The container of this material must be properly sealed to prevent "flowing and leaking" of the powdered Cryston.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Acquired by grinding Origocrust Powder. Can be used to produce other materials.
Techno-Protocol grinding is used to prepare this dense powdered form of Origocrust with particles almost invisible to the unaided eye.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Acquired by grinding Originium Powder. This stable material can be used to produce other items.
The UWST Originium cultivation project initially attempted "powder seeding" but the resulting crystal clusters would often lose their activity quickly.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Acquired by grinding Carbon Powder. Can be used to produce other materials.
Techno-Protocol grinding is used to create this uniquely fine powdered preparation of Carbon.
Acquired by grinding Citrome Powder. Can be used to produce other items.
Techno-Protocol grinding is used to make this Citrome preparation with an extremely bitter taste.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Acquired by grinding Buckflower Powder. Can be used to produce other items.
Techno-Protocol grinding is used to make this buckflower preparation with "strangely" improved pharmaceutical activity.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Powdered form of Aketine. Can be used to produce other materials.
It is easier to extract target materials and substances from shredded Aketine.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Powdered Sandleaf. Can be used to produce other materials.
Shredding releases the abrasive grit from sandleaf tissues for industrial use.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Shredded and powdered Ferrium. Can be used to produce other items.
Ferrium can be shredded to yield a processed product with improved homogeneity.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Shredded and powdered Amethyst Fibers. Can be used to produce other items.
Amethyst can be shredded to yield a processed product with improved homogeneity.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A stabilized form of powdered Originium. Can be used to produce other materials.
Endfield made use of Techno-Protocol to create a stable form of Originium Powder.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A stabilized form of powdered Originium. Can be used to produce other materials.
Endfield made use of Techno-Protocol to create a stable form of Originium Powder.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Powdered form of Carbon. Can be used to produce other materials.
Carbon can be shredded to yield a powdered product.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A material produced by a specialized facility. Can be used for repairing special facilities or for trading with various outposts.
A novel Originium-based material acquired by fusing the mineral with Feranmut power. This material fuels life and prosperity in Wuling.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A fertilizer made by mixing burdo-muck with Liquid Xiranite. Can be used to fertilize a planting plot during the growth phase to increase its harvest yield.
Processed burdo-muck fertilizer. Can be used to fertilize planting plots.
"Plop a bar of Bumper-Rich to your beautiful fields! Who'd say no to 88 tons per acre yield?"
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A high quality material prepared by refining Dense Ferrium Powder. Can be used to produce other items.
Steel manufactured by Endfield Industries has been subject to multiple processing steps. It has been included in the list of Grade 2 Reference Materials in the
Talos-II Blight Resistance Standards of Industrial Metals
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A high quality material prepared by refining Cryston Powder. Can be used to produce other items.
Cryston Fibers can undergo various processing steps to create a material for transmitting data and communication signals.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A high quality material prepared by refining Dense Origocrust Powder. Can be used to produce other materials.
The Packed Origocrust is a result of multiple processing steps and looks just like a piece of precious "gemstone".
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
An advanced material prepared by refining Dense Carbon Powder. Can be used to make other materials.
Multiple processing steps have achieved a delicate balance for this brick of elemental carbon.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A metallic ingot made by refining Ferrium Ore. Can be used to produce other materials.
A metal of Talos-II that is remarkably similar to Oriron. No one could explain the similarity.
Fibers made by refining Amethyst Ore. Can be used to produce other items.
Factory-drawn Amethyst fibers exhibit vastly increased strength.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Crystalline crust acquired by delamination of Originium. Can be used to produce other items.
The thickness of the crystalline crust encasing an Originium formation (referred to as Origocrust) is often used to determine the age of the Originium vein.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Carbon captured from refinery incineration of plant matter. Can be used to produce other materials.
During the war, Endfield Industries managed to optimize their waste emission carbon capture technology.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Xiranite dissolved in Clean Water. Can be used to produce other materials. Often filled in containers and loaded on jet drones for Liquid Xiranite spraying and elimination of Active Blight in Wuling.

When people first hear about aqueous Xiranite solutions, most would regard its production as a simple process. However, a user without proper equipment nor techniques would only arrive at a useless product — one that is hardly any different than sand mixed in water.
A solution of Yazhen. It can be processed and used for producing medicine.
A solution prepared to maximize the effects of yazhen extract.
A solution of Jincao. It can be processed and used for producing medicine.
A solution prepared to maximize the effects of jincao extract.
Seeds of wild Amber Rice. Cultivate them in available farm plots to raise the crop.
Amber Rice seeds carefully selected for their generous size and vitality.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Seeds of wild Redjade Ginseng. Cultivate them in available farm plots to raise the crop.
Redjade Ginseng seeds carefully selected for their generous size and vitality.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Seeds of wild Tartpepper. Cultivate them in available farm plots to raise the crop.
Tartpepper seeds carefully selected for their generous size and vitality.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Seeds of wild Reed Rye. Cultivate them in available farm plots to raise the crop.
Reed Rye seeds carefully selected for their generous size and vitality.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
This crop can only be grown with fine cultivation. Can be directly used in making quality food and pharmaceuticals.
This rounded, jade-like endosperm of the Amber Rice is also regarded as "amber pearls".
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
This crop can only be grown with fine cultivation. Can be directly used in making quality food and pharmaceuticals.
Redjade Ginseng is a mild herb often consumed by people in Hongshan as a fortifying supplement.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
This crop can only be grown with fine cultivation. Can be directly used in making quality food and pharmaceuticals.
The leaves of this plant are considered the "most bitter herbs of the frontier".
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
This crop can only be grown with fine cultivation. Can be directly used in making quality food and pharmaceuticals.
Reed Rye is a hardy cereal with an impressive yield. It is the staple food of most settlers in the frontier.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A special plant that only grows in the wild. Shred it to acquire materials for making food or other products.
The thorny leaves of the yazhen plant can be used for angling. A few simple steps and bending would create a very effective angling hook.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A special plant that only grows in the wild. Shred it to acquire materials for making food or other products.
Some people in Wuling believe that the jincao plant symbolizes hardiness. Many pieces of local literature use the plant as a metaphor for toughness and survival.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A plant with special fruits that grows in the wild. Can be used for treatment and the making of fine food and medicine (Fine F&M). Umbralines do not provide seeds.

Around 12 major Citrome cultivars are currently sold in the markets of the Civilization Band. They come in all sorts of names and offer different juiciness and sugar content. However, they are all Citromes.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A plant with extremely brightly colored flowers that grows in the wild. Can be used for treatment and the making of fine food and medicine (Fine F&M). Firebuckles do not provide seeds.
Buckflowers bloom in mid-April. Plants with the most fiery-red flowers would be called Firebuckles.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Pure and clean water. It has plenty of uses in the AIC Factory.
It has always been the source of life.
Common wood that can be gathered in the Mossfield region. Can be used to produce other materials.
Let's chop down some trees first.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Yazhen seed can be acquired by a Seed-Picking Unit. Cultivate it with plenty of Clean Water to grow the plant.
Yazhen seeds have tiny hooks that aid in their attachment to animal fur for dispersion.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A flowering plant that can be gathered from the wilderness or planted in the AIC. Shred it to make medication or materials for other products.
The yazhen plant is an extremely hardy grass native to the Wuling region. The plant produces a rather beautiful bloom during summer.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Jincao seed can be acquired by a Seed-Picking Unit. Cultivate it with plenty of Clean Water to grow the plant.
Children in the frontier enjoy blowing two jincao leaves placed together to give off a sound that resembles a roaring beast.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A plant that can be gathered from the wilderness or planted in the AIC. Shred it to make food or materials for other products.
The jincao plant is commonly known as the "silkstar grass", named after the star-like flowers of the plant. The plant usually grows on shaded hillslopes.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
An Aketine seed.  Can be cultivated into a new adult plant.
Nitrates can be extracted from Aketine. Scientists believe this is caused by the absorption of rainwater with excessively high levels of Blighted materials.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A plant that can be gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the AIC Factory. It contains flammable and explosive substances and can be used to make Industrial Explosives.
Aketine is a species of flora native to Talos-II. It usually grows at places with unique soil composition.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Seed of the Sandleaf. Can be cultivated to grow the plant.
The history of
wilderness crafting
can be traced back nearly a century ago. People forced to live in the wildlands had to do everything they could to survive.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A plant with extremely rough leaves that can be gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the AIC. Shred it to get a powdered preparation usable in industrial grinding.
The Hannabit Circuit proved themselves as true masters of
wilderness crafting
and would use all sorts of natural materials to aid in their crafting.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Seed of the Citrome. Can be cultivated to grow the plant.
Hobby botanists and gardeners of the Civilization Band once voted for the
Most Common Plants in the Wildlands.
Buckflower and Citromes both made it to the top 10 of the list.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A fruit that can be gathered from the wilderness or planted in the AIC. Shred it to make food or materials for other products.

Citromes are part of the botanical genus of Zitros that usually grows in the central continental region of Talos-II. The species and cultivars grown in the Civilization Band tend to yield large fruits with tough, fibrous flesh.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Seed of the Buckflower. Can be cultivated to grow the plant.
The Buckflower earned its name as it was first found under a natural arch that resembled a bucking, jumping beast.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A plant with brightly colored flowers that can be gathered from the wilderness or planted in the AIC. Shred it to make medication or materials for other products.
When the War just began, a Hongshan recon team discovered a plant with brightly colored flowers. Extracts of this plant can be used as an anti-hemorrhagic agent.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A natural-occurring metal complex with blue coloration. It can be processed with the AIC.
Many theories have been proposed about the origins of Ferrium. Before the age of industrial metallurgy, Ferrium was the most common metal that people used.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
An ore containing a purple colored crystalline material. It can be processed with the AIC.
Amethyst has a wide range of uses in precision manufacturing. The crystal is one of the essential basic materials in the electronic industry of Talos-II.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A raw formation of Originium unearthed in its most natural form. This can be processed in the AIC.
A basic mineral necessary to support most industrial production.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Walking Chrysopolises. Can be used at a depot node for Pack and Deliver.
A unique alloy that can be acquired by cutting out Aggeloid "shield armor". The Tianshi have realized that the metal is extremely dense and has properties similar to Hetonite.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Spotted Rakerbeast. Can be used at a depot node for Pack and Deliver.

Fine, fluffy fur collected from near the frontal paws of the rakerbeast. A rakerbeast would spend a lot of time grooming the "iridescent" part of its fur. You can even see threads of residual blood of unfortunate prey within the gaps.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Axe Armorbeasts. Can be used at a depot node for Pack and Deliver.
The massive horn of the Axe Armorbeast broken during intense battle. Warriors would only drop their weapons when slain. The same applies to Axe Armorbeasts.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Effigies. Can be used at a depot node for Pack and Deliver.
Strange minerals can sometimes be gathered around an Effigy's halo. The stones exhibit excellent thermal conductance. Heat sinks made out of them are quite popular on the market.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Bonekrusher Ballistas. Can be used at a depot node for Pack and Deliver.

A super heavy crossbow "soaked" in hazefyre for many years. Given safety considerations, using this as a weapon is NOT advised. However, wildly unconventional weapons designed by Landbreakers are highly sought after by various collectors.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Bonekrusher Executioners. Can be used at a depot node for Pack and Deliver.
The dried bloodstains serve as a proof of its past. The terror and zealotry it once personified are now yours.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Quillbeasts. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).
A tender piece of liver. Almost every tissue of the quillbeast is mildly toxic. Its liver is the only exception.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Tidewalkers. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).
A black liquid with an incredibly sweet and cloying taste. The primary contents identified through analyses are currently referred to as "artificial sweeteners".
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Bonekrusher Arsonists. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).

A special backup fuel usually equipped and used by Bonekrusher Arsonists. It seems to be beast fat mixed with a certain dye. During periods of slim production and/or raiding, Landbreakers would occasionally acquire lipids from "local sources".
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Bonekrusher Pyromancers. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).

Crushed beast cartilage. Bonekrusher Pyromancers would usually use this as "fuel" for their denstacks. However, hazefyre is not produced by burning cartilage. Rather, the pyromancers burn the beasts' remains as a means of convincing themselves of their victory at hand.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Tunneling Nidwyrms. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).

Nidwyrms have "whiskers" growing on their heads. These appendages appear like tree roots and serve as sensory organs. These whiskers are naturally shed when spring gives way to summer. Although they offer limited nutritional value, they have an incredible texture.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Waterlamps. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).
The glowing organ of the waterlamp. The fluids within it can be used as an edible dye. Essential for the final garnish for the famous Wuling dessert resembling the Tianshi Pillar.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Hedrons or Prisms. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).
This natural sparkling water has a strange fizz. It is cool, refreshing, and reinvigorating when consumed.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Mudflows. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).

A strange form of water. Although wholly composed of dihydrogen monoxide, it felt strangely gel-like to the touch. Perhaps the process of Aggeloid formation has induced strange changes to the properties of this material.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Falsewings. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).

Aggagrit that can be occasionally harvested from Aggeloid "elytrons". Its flavor differs from other types of Aggagrit and many find it incredibly difficult to describe. Most would eventually arrive at this conclusion: "Maybe this is the taste of the sky."
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Bonekrusher Vanguards. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).

Extremely poor quality flour of unknown ingredients. Overall color is a strange, grayish hue. The Landbreakers seem to have added some dried fungal powder to this mix as the sack of flour exudes a moldy smell.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Pincerbeasts, Acid Originium Slugs, and Firemist Originium Slugs. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).
This meat is rich in protein but must be meticulously prepared to remove all active Originium dust and particles from the tissues.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot. (change accordingly)
Gatherable from defeated Wild Tuskbeasts or Bonekrusher Ripptusks. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).
A piece of fillet. It seems pretty fresh.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Road Plunderers or Grove Archers. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).
Freshly harvested young bamboo sprouts from Qingbo Stockade. Tender, refreshing, and delicious. This quality food ingredient is famed throughout Talos-II.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Hazefyre Tuskbeasts and Hazefyre Claws. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).
Dried, marinated jerky that looks obviously abnormal due to prolonged exposure to hazefyre. Eating this is NOT advised. However, you may consider using it as backup firewood.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from defeated Bonekrusher Raiders, Bonekrusher Ballistas, or Bonekrusher Infiltrators. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).

A can of dried powder with bits of seed chaffs and coats. The powder is slightly toxic and the Landbreakers seem to consume it like an "instant energy drink mix". However, there is no discounting the possibility that the powder might be used as seasoning.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from Stings or Heavy Stings. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).
Strange minerals can sometimes be gathered from the Aggeloi's "energy-focusing tail stingers". They can be used as a food marinate to give it special flavors.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Gatherable from Rams or Heavy Rams. Can be used to make fine food and medicine (Fine F&M).
Strange minerals can sometimes be gathered from the Aggeloi's "ramming horns". They can be used as a food marinade to give it special flavors.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Burdenbeast dung. Can be used as a fertilizer on a planting plot during the growth phase to shorten its growth phase.
Burdo-Muck provides all sorts of nutrients to the soil.
If you see a burdenbeast in the wilderness, give it a gentle pat to get yourself a huge, mucking pat of hot, steaming surprise.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A small flying Originium bug with high energy content. Can be used to produce other materials.
A Talosian creature affected by Originium. These little bugs can glow and give off a bit of heat.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A small flying Originium bug. Can be used to produce other materials.
A Talosian creature affected by Originium. These little bugs can give off a gentle glow.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
Use this at Severe Energy Alluvium sites of Wuling. It can pre-engrave the stats of weapon essences acquired.
A permit for expanded engraving produced in Wuling. Engraving must be done in a strictly sealed environment to prevent uncontrollable corruption between different essences.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
Use this at Severe Energy Alluvium sites of Valley IV. It can pre-engrave the stats of weapon essences acquired.
A permit for expanded engraving produced in Valley IV. Engraving must be done in a strictly sealed environment to prevent uncontrollable corruption between different essences.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A Chrysodendra tuber that can be cultivated into a new adult plant.
Chrysodendra can be used for operator skill upgrades at skill levels 4 to 6.
A Chrysodendra tuber carefully selected for survivability.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A Vitrodendra tuber that can be cultivated into a new adult plant.
Vitrodendra can be used for operator skill upgrades at skill levels 7 to 9.
A Vitrodendra tuber carefully selected for survivability.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A special plant gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Extracts of this plant can be used for operator skill upgrades at skill levels 4 to 6.
Dendras are plants native to Talos-II. Farming dendras can be quite a challenge.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A special plant gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Extracts of this plant can be used for operator skill upgrades at skill levels 7 to 9.
Accidental consumption of dendras may lead to high blood pressure, faster heart rates, nauseousness, and vomiting.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A special mineral gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Tunes Lv.20 weapons.
Nyxites are also known as the "ancient mineral" and symbolize an age of destruction of such a scale never witnessed by humanity. They come in various hues.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A Kalkodendra tuber that can be cultivated into a new adult plant.
Kalkodendra can be used for operator skill upgrades at skill levels 1 to 3.
A Kalkodendra tuber carefully selected for survivability.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A seeding cluster of Kalkonyx that can be used to grow a new crystal.
Kalkonyxes can tune Lv.20 weapons.
A Kalkonyx seeding cluster selected for its usefulness in crystal nucleation.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A special mineral gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Tunes Lv.40 weapons.
Humans have yet to figure out how nyxites form. This secret of Talos-II has yet to unveil itself.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A special plant gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Extracts of this plant can be used for operator skill upgrades at skill levels 1 to 3.
Dendra extracts were once used as emergency medication.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A seeding cluster of Auronyx that can be used to grow a new crystal.
Auronyxes can tune Lv.40 weapons.
An Auronyx seeding cluster selected for its usefulness in crystal nucleation.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A Ruby Bolete mycelial mass that can be cultivated into a new adult fungus.
Red Boletes can promote Lv.60 operators.
A Ruby Bolete mycelial mass carefully selected for its activity.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A special mineral gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Tunes Lv.60 weapons.
People usually name nyxite crystals based on their color (Kalkonyx, Auronyx, and Umbronyx) but these differences are just caused by the extent of oxidation.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A special fungus gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Promotes Lv.60 operators.

Every year, many frontier settlers would suffer multiple organ failure caused by ruby bolete poisoning. This often results from misjudging the edible stage of the fungus. Eating the fungus in ruby stage is extremely dangerous.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A seeding cluster of Umbronyx that can be used to grow a new crystal.
Umbronyxes can tune Lv.60 weapons.
An Umbronyx seeding cluster selected for its usefulness in crystal nucleation.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A Red Bolete mycelial mass that can be cultivated into a new adult fungus.
Red Boletes can promote Lv.40 operators.
A Red Bolete mycelial mass carefully selected for its activity.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A special fungus gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Promotes Lv.80 operators.
Bloodcap is likely to be a variant of a carnivorous fungus mutated by ambient Blight. Nutrients consumed by the fungus would course through its mycelia.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A special fungus gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Promotes Lv.40 operators.
Ruby boletes with darker hues provide extracts with greater pharmaceutical values. This is the red stage.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A Bloodcap mycelial mass that can be cultivated into a new adult fungus.
Bloodcaps can promote certain Lv.80 or higher operators.
A Bloodcap mycelial mass carefully selected for its activity.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A special plant gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Extracts of this plant can be used in operator skill mastery.
Some scientists believe that certain Blight conditions can transform any bush or shrubs into the Blighted Jadeleaves.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A Pink Bolete mycelial mass that can be cultivated into a new adult fungus.
Pink Boletes can promote Lv.20 operators.
A Pink Bolete mycelial mass carefully selected for its activity.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A Blighted Jadeleaf tuber that can be cultivated into a new adult plant.
Blighted Jadeleaf can be used for operator skill mastery.
A Blighted Jadeleaf tuber carefully selected for survivability.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A special fungus gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Promotes Lv.20 operators.
Toxicity of the ruby bolete would already be extremely high when the sporocarp reaches a certain size and starts acquiring a reddish hue. This is the pink stage.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A special mineral gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Tunes Lv.80 weapons.
Scientists postulate that Igneosites are metamorphic forms of nyxite subject to volcanic eruptions followed by Blight exposure.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A rare material that upgrades essence skills.

A special coolant gel used in the essence etching process. Results of etching are extremely sensitive to temperature changes. This process thus requires the use of coolant gels, which can be extremely expensive to make.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A seeding cluster of Igneosite that can be used to grow a new crystal.
Igneosites can tune Lv.80 weapons.
An Igneosite seeding cluster selected for its usefulness in crystal nucleation.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A material that tunes weapons.
A heavy casting die made using rare materials. Techno-Protocol activation lets this die enhance general material strengths in weapons.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A special fungus gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Promotes Lv.80 operators.

A common fungus that proliferated in various regions of Talos-II after the Cosmic Gate broke down. Terran fowlbeasts brought to the new world exhibit a special preference for these surprisingly hard "local mushrooms". Cosmagaric also disperse their spores by attaching them to the long feathers of the fowls.
This item is placed om the Backpack and/or Depot.
A material that tunes weapons.
A heavy casting die made using rare materials. Techno-Protocol activation lets this die enhance general material strengths in weapons.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A Cosmagaric mycelial mass that can be cultivated into a new adult fungus.
Cosmagarics can promote certain Lv.80 or higher operators.
A Cosmagaric mycelial mass carefully selected for its activity.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A material that provides a lot of weapon EXP.
A single-use toolset with AIC technology. Provides automated in-depth inspection of the entire weapon structure. Also repairs internal material damage and significantly enhances weapon performance.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A special plant gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Extracts of this plant can be used in operator skill mastery.
An ancient plant that looks and feels like a rock. The False Aggela takes on the appearance of tiny Aggeloi when growing. Life on Talos-II never stopped evolving, even during humanity's silence.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A material that gives decent weapon EXP.
A single-use mini-kit with AIC technology. Provides automated inspection of the entire weapon structure. Also repairs internal material damage and enhances weapon performance.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A False Aggela tuber that can be cultivated into a new adult plant.
False Aggela can be used for operator skill mastery.
A False Aggela tuber carefully selected for survivability.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A material that provides some weapon EXP.
A single-use micro-device with AIC technology. Provides automated inspection of weapon parts. Also repairs internal material damage and slightly enhances weapon performance.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A special mineral gathered from the wilderness or cultivated in the Growth Chamber.
Tunes Lv.80 weapons.

A mineral formation created by extremely complex hydrological changes. Wulingstone was first discovered by Hongshan Tianshi conducting hydrological surveys at the Wuling region. Under the influence of Æther, sediments carried by water would settle at certain depths and eventually form Wulingstone. These rocks are useful in fluviochronology and the study of local hydrological history.
This item is placed in the Backpack and/or Depot.
A rare material that upgrades operator skill mastery.
The perseverance of life and continuation of civilization have left their marks upon this place.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A seeding cluster of Wulingstone that can be used to grow a new crystal.
Wulingstones can tune Lv.80 weapons.
A Wulingstone seeding cluster selected for its usefulness in crystal nucleation.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A rare material for operator promotion, weapon tuning, and operator skill mastery.

An ion trap that might be leveraging its innate Æther properties. The lattice ignores temporal factors and is capable of detecting and screening naturally occurring tachyon beams. Since the people of Talos-II are still incapable of activating and firing tachyons, the lattice is thus essential for any experiments involving random outcome solidification.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A rare material for operator promotion, weapon tuning, and operator skill mastery.

A synthetic item for detecting and verifying various field forces and actions at a distance. The tube usually comprises a time crystal sealed in a static vessel, and uses observable regression properties of the photons to achieve its purpose. Though typically stable, users should avoid breaking the vessel and stay out of areas between a pair of linked tubes.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A material that upgrades operator skills.
A special crystal polyhedron manufactured using Techno-Protocol and advanced crystallization processes. Often combined with other devices to improve physiological absorption of enhancement materials.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A material that upgrades operator skills.
A special crystal prism manufactured using Techno-Protocol and standard crystallization processes. Often combined with other devices to improve physiological absorption of enhancement materials.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A material that promotes operators to E3 or E4.
A special disk manufactured using Techno-Protocol and advanced date etching processes. Often used with other devices to effectively alleviate physical fatigue of operators during training.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A material that promotes operators to E1 or E2.
A special disk manufactured using Techno-Protocol and standard data etching processes. Often used with other devices to effectively alleviate physical fatigue of operators during training.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A material that provides decent EXP for leveling up Lv.61-99 operators.

A data storage unit developed by Endfield Industries. It is compatible with various data access devices. New operators can use it to effectively acquire combat knowledge. Thanks to support offered Cabal of Tranquility, this carrier features data compression technology and thus stores even more information.
This item is placed in Valuables Stash.
A material that provides some EXP for leveling up Lv.61-99 operators.
A data storage unit developed by Endfield Industries. It is compatible with various data access devices. New operators can use it to effectively acquire combat knowledge.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A rare material for operator promotion, weapon tuning, and operator skill mastery.

A hypothetical condensed matter in fluid state. Though it could be synthesized and the optical properties of the resulting materials could be observed, the actual structure and state of the fluid have yet to be successfully measured. The fluid effectively suppresses Originium sublimation in all Depths that could be artificially created, making it an extraordinarily effective filler.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A rare material for operator promotion, weapon tuning, and operator skill mastery.

A device that is extremely sensitive to ambient Originium. It can boost Originium sensitivity of all weapons and devices within a significantly large area. The Triphasic Nanoflake is a key component for containing and storing the Arts, and offers better performance in non-realspace environments compared to the older Bipolar Nanoflakes.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
A rare material for operator promotion, weapon tuning, and operator skill mastery.

A novel and artificial alloy of D32 steel and D64 steel that cannot exist naturally. D96 steel has inherited the excellent strength and Arts conduction properties of its constituents. It also features better performance in areas with unstable Originium activities. The material is still being experimented upon, with Sample 4 offering the best cost performance index.
This item is placed in the Valuables Stash.
